summary: Tested iteration_13 Contact-section admin editing. Backend: 29 contact keys are all seeded and returned by /api/content; admin PUT/GET persistence works. Frontend public /contact renders correctly with all driven labels, placeholders, sidebar labels and validation messages. CRITICAL bug: Admin Website Text page does NOT show a 'Contact page' group — ALL 162 content fields render under a single 'general' group, defeating the whole iteration goal. Root cause is in the snapshot replay code.

critical.issue: All 162 content rows are grouped under 'general' instead of 'brand/nav/home/pricing/journeys/faqs/gallery/contact/footer/seo'. The seeded `group` field is being wiped by the on-startup snapshot replay. As a result, /admin/website-text shows one giant 'general' group with no 'Contact page' (or any other) section.

ui_bugs.issue: No 'Contact page' group card rendered. Only one 'general' group with 162 fields. Group labels (Brand / Navigation / Home page / ... / Contact page / Footer / SEO) are completely missing because the API response has only the 'general' key.

action_items: Fix the snapshot replay so the `group` (and `type`/`label`) field is preserved. Two viable fixes: (a) Re-save /app/backend/seed_data/site_snapshot.json so each `content` entry includes the original `group`, `type`, `label` fields (currently every row is just {key,value}). The snapshot writer in server.py is stripping these. (b) Defensively in _apply_snapshot (server.py:2279-2282), look up the existing row's `group/type/label` and preserve them if the snapshot entry is missing them, OR fall back to inferring group from the key prefix.

After the snapshot fix, restart backend so the DB rows get the correct `group` value. Then re-verify GET /api/admin/content returns >=10 groups (brand, nav, home, pricing, journeys, faqs, gallery, contact, footer, seo) and that /admin/website-text shows a 'Contact page' card with 29 editable fields.

Bonus (also worth fixing): PUT /api/admin/content (server.py:846) does not set `group` on upsert. New keys created via the admin UI will have no group and land in 'general'. Either require the client to pass `group`, or infer group from `key.split('.')[0]`.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:2279-2282 — `_apply_snapshot` does `db.content.update_one({key},{ $set: entry })` with a snapshot entry that only contains {key,value}, silently destroying the existing `group/type/label` metadata of every content row. This is the root cause of the 'one giant general group' bug.

Snapshot writer (presumably `schedule_snapshot`/save logic) is serialising content rows as just {key,value} (162/162 entries lack the `group` field). The snapshot must round-trip group/type/label too, otherwise every deploy/restart corrupts the admin grouping.
server.py:846 `admin_update_content` — upsert path does not set `group` on insert of brand-new keys. Combine with the inference-from-prefix idea above (`key.split('.')[0]`).
seed_data_creation: None — only PUT/GET on first_name_label which was reverted to 'First name' after the assertion.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the snapshot/grouping bug, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_contact_admin.py — the `test_admin_content_contains_all_contact_keys` test is the one that asserts the 'contact' group exists. Also re-open /admin/website-text and confirm a 'Contact page' card is present with 29 fields and that the 'Preview on site' pill links to /contact. Public /contact rendering is already verified working.
rca_of_the_issue: Symptom: /admin/website-text shows everything under one 'general' group. Reproduction: 1) GET /api/admin/content with admin Bearer token → response has a single 'general' key with 162 items. 2) Inspect /app/backend/seed_data/site_snapshot.json content array → every entry has only {key,value}, no group/type/label. Cause: on backend startup the snapshot is replayed (server.py:2402 `_apply_snapshot(... reason='auto-migration-on-startup')`) and overwrites each content row with `$set: entry` (line 2282) where `entry` is the stripped-down {key,value} from snapshot.json. This wipes the `group` field that DEFAULT_CONTENT had originally seeded. Mitigation: see action_items — preserve metadata in the snapshot writer AND defensively guard `_apply_snapshot` against missing fields.
